assumptions: all terms are dimensionless finite simulator observables
larger index means greater modelled systems dysregulation
clinical labels are not inferred from this simulator quantity
specs.canonical_statement: Pathology is bounded to a computable systems-state index combining free-energy accumulation, synchronisation loss, criticality deviation, and error-correction failure.
specs.claim_boundary: simulator-only systems metric; not clinical evidence or medical advice
executable_validation_targets: verify pathology index increases with free-energy accumulation
verify coherence loss increases pathology index
verify QEC failure probability contributes monotonically
null_controls: healthy-baseline control must return lower index
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > Applied SCPN: Pathology, Technology, and Anomalies
validation_targets: verify pathology index increases with free-energy accumulation
verify coherence loss increases pathology index
verify QEC failure probability contributes monotonically

assumptions: sigma is a finite positive branching or response-ratio proxy
clinical examples in the manuscript remain interpretive context only
classification thresholds are explicit and tested
specs.canonical_statement: Deviation from quasicriticality is promoted only as a sigma classifier: sigma > 1 is supercritical, sigma < 1 is subcritical, and sigma near 1 is quasicritical.
specs.claim_boundary: simulator-only systems metric; not clinical evidence or medical advice
executable_validation_targets: verify sigma above tolerance classifies as supercritical
verify sigma below tolerance classifies as subcritical
verify sigma-neutral tolerance classifies as quasicritical
null_controls: sigma-neutral control must not be classified as pathological
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > Aetiology of Disorder: > Dissonance (Free Energy Accumulation): Sustained accumulation of Prediction Errors (EL) in the HPC architecture. PathologyIndex∝FGlobal​. > Deviation from Criticality: Shifts away from σ=1. Supercriticality (σ > 1, e.g., mania, seizures); Subcriticality (σ < 1, e.g., depression, coma).
validation_targets: verify sigma above tolerance classifies as supercritical
verify sigma below tolerance classifies as subcritical
verify sigma-neutral tolerance classifies as quasicritical

assumptions: updates are small finite simulator controls
restoration score compares before and after states only
therapeutic wording is not clinical evidence or medical advice
specs.canonical_statement: Therapeutic restoration is bounded to simulator control objectives: reduce prediction-error/free-energy accumulation, tune sigma toward 1, and increase phase synchronisation.
specs.claim_boundary: simulator-only systems metric; not clinical evidence or medical advice
executable_validation_targets: verify restoration update reduces pathology index
verify sigma update moves toward one without overshoot hidden by clipping
verify synchronisation update increases bounded order parameter
null_controls: zero-update control must leave pathology index unchanged
wrong-direction update control must increase pathology index
specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > Aetiology of Disorder: > Dissonance (Free Energy Accumulation): Sustained accumulation of Prediction Errors (EL) in the HPC architecture. PathologyIndex∝FGlobal​. > Fragmentation: Failure of integration (e.g., L5 Self fragmentation in dissociation; L11 societal polarisation).
validation_targets: verify restoration update reduces pathology index
verify sigma update moves toward one without overshoot hidden by clipping
verify synchronisation update increases bounded order parameter

summary.policy: P0R06197-P0R06205 are promoted as source-covered validation specifications only. Pathology and therapeutic language is bounded to finite simulator systems metrics and is not clinical evidence.
